---
title: "Stage 2 — Bayesian Hierarchical Model (INLA)"
author: "Valiao, Syrelle Kaye D."
date: "2026-01-29"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
    keep_tex: false
fontsize: 11pt
geometry: margin=1in
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{amsmath}
  - \usepackage{amssymb}
  - \usepackage{graphicx}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, eval = TRUE,
                      fig.pos = "H")

# ── RERUN CONTROL ────────────────────────────────────────
RERUN_INLA_MODELS <- FALSE     # Change to TRUE if you modify model specs
RERUN_BYM <- FALSE             # Change to TRUE if you modify BYM architecture
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Load CEVAE outputs first (prerequisite)
cevae_workspace <- "~/thesis_char/Thesis/cevae_workspace.RData"
inla_workspace <- "~/thesis_char/Thesis/inla_workspace.RData"

if (file.exists(cevae_workspace)) {
  load(cevae_workspace)
  cat("✓ CEVAE workspace loaded\n")
}

# Then load existing INLA results if available
if (file.exists(inla_workspace) && !RERUN_INLA_MODELS && !RERUN_BYM) {
  load(inla_workspace)
  cat("✓ INLA workspace loaded (RERUN flags = FALSE)\n")
}

set.seed(0942)
```

```{r inla-models, eval = RERUN_INLA_MODELS, cache=FALSE}
library(INLA)
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(0942)

inla.setOption(num.threads = 10)
inla.setOption(smtp = "taucs")

inla_input <- readRDS("cevae_inla_input.RDS")

# Spatial precision prior (PC prior) 
# P(sigma > 1) = 0.01: 99% confident spatial SD < 1 log-UIC unit
pc_prior_spatial <- list(
  prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1, 0.01))
)

prepare_dat <- function(dat_m) {
  
  active_cols <- c("z_mean_1", "z_mean_7", "z_mean_10")
  all_z_cols   <- paste0("z_mean_", 1:10)
  
  dat_m %>%
    mutate(
      # Treatment: numeric 0/1
      urban_bin     = as.numeric(urban == "Urban"),
      
      # z_std: active dims (1, 7, 10) row-mean, then standardized — M1
      z_active_mean = rowMeans(across(all_of(active_cols))),
      z_std         = as.numeric(scale(z_active_mean)),
      
      # z_std_full: all 10 dims row-mean, then standardized — M2 sensitivity
      z_all_mean    = rowMeans(across(all_of(all_z_cols))),
      z_std_full    = as.numeric(scale(z_all_mean)),
      
      # Covariates
      age_std       = as.numeric(scale(age)),
      sex_bin       = as.numeric(sex == "Female"),
      educ_std      = as.numeric(scale(as.numeric(as.character(education)))),
      year_fact     = factor(survey_year),
      
      # Spatial ID: province first, fallback to region
      prov_id       = as.integer(factor(province_code)),
      region_id     = as.integer(factor(region)),
      spatial_id    = if (all(is.na(prov_id))) region_id else prov_id
    )
}

# M1: Main CEVAE-adjusted model (active latent dims 4, 7, 9)
formula_m1 <- log_uic ~
  urban_bin + z_std +
  age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
  f(spatial_id, model = "iid", hyper = pc_prior_spatial)

# M2: Sensitivity — all 10 latent dims
formula_m2 <- log_uic ~
  urban_bin + z_std_full +
  age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
  f(spatial_id, model = "iid", hyper = pc_prior_spatial)

# M3: Naive baseline — no latent z
formula_m3 <- log_uic ~
  urban_bin +
  age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
  f(spatial_id, model = "iid", hyper = pc_prior_spatial)

formulas <- list(M1 = formula_m1, M2 = formula_m2, M3 = formula_m3)

# Fit all models across all imputations 
inla_fits <- list()

# ── CONFOUNDING ADJUSTMENT (Independent of Survey Weights) ─
# Model includes:
#   • weights = survey design correction
#   • z_std = latent confounding proxy (CEVAE)
#   • age_std, sex_bin, educ_std, year_fact = measured confounders
# These are NOT redundant; weights address sampling design,
# confounders address causal confounding.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── SURVEY WEIGHTING ────────────────────────────────────────
# weights = fwgti_natl2_var / mean(fwgti_natl2_var, na.rm=TRUE)
# Source: ENNS 2018-2019 National sampling weights
# Purpose: Correct for differential sampling probability by region/strata
# Note: These are NOT confounding-related weights (not propensity weights)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

for (mod_name in names(formulas)) {
  
  cat(sprintf("Fitting model %s\n", mod_name))

  inla_fits[[mod_name]] <- vector("list", 5)
  
  for (m in 1:5) {
    cat(sprintf("  Imputation %d/5...", m))
    
    dat_m <- inla_input %>%
      filter(imputation_id == m) %>%
      prepare_dat()
    
    inla_fits[[mod_name]][[m]] <- inla(
      formulas[[mod_name]],
      family            = "gaussian",
      data              = dat_m,
      weights           = dat_m$weight,
      control.compute   = list(
        dic              = TRUE,
        waic             = TRUE,
        config           = TRUE,
        return.marginals = TRUE
      ),
      control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE)
    )
    
    cat(sprintf(" DIC = %.2f | WAIC = %.2f\n",
                inla_fits[[mod_name]][[m]]$dic$dic,
                inla_fits[[mod_name]][[m]]$waic$waic))
  }
}

# MATE extraction + Rubin's Rules
pool_mate <- function(fits, coef_name = "urban_bin", n_obs) {
  
  # Verify coefficient name exists
  fixed_names <- rownames(fits[[1]]$summary.fixed)
  if (!coef_name %in% fixed_names) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "'%s' not found in summary.fixed. Available: %s",
      coef_name, paste(fixed_names, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  
  # Extract per-imputation estimates
  mate_tbl <- lapply(1:5, function(m) {
    row <- fits[[m]]$summary.fixed[coef_name, ]
    data.frame(
      imputation_id = m,
      Q  = row$mean,
      U  = row$sd^2,
      lo = row$`0.025quant`,
      hi = row$`0.975quant`
    )
  }) %>% bind_rows()
  
  # Rubin's Rules
  M       <- 5
  Q_bar   <- mean(mate_tbl$Q)
  U_bar   <- mean(mate_tbl$U)
  B       <- var(mate_tbl$Q)
  T_var   <- U_bar + (1 + 1/M) * B
  SE_pool <- sqrt(T_var)
  lambda  <- (1 + 1/M) * B / T_var
  
  # Degrees of freedom (Barnard & Rubin 1999)
  n_par  <- nrow(fits[[1]]$summary.fixed)
  df_old <- (M - 1) / lambda^2
  df_obs <- (n_obs - n_par) * (1 - lambda)
  df_adj <- (df_old * df_obs) / (df_old + df_obs)
  t_crit <- qt(0.975, df = df_adj)
  
  data.frame(
    MATE         = round(Q_bar, 4),
    SE           = round(SE_pool, 4),
    CI_low       = round(Q_bar - t_crit * SE_pool, 4),
    CI_high      = round(Q_bar + t_crit * SE_pool, 4),
    MATE_exp     = round(exp(Q_bar), 4),
    df           = round(df_adj, 1),
    lambda       = round(lambda, 4),
    per_imp_ests = paste(round(mate_tbl$Q, 4), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Get n_obs from the prepared data (same for all imputations)
n_obs_per_imp <- inla_input %>%
  filter(imputation_id == 1) %>%
  prepare_dat() %>%
  nrow()

cat(sprintf("n_obs per imputation: %d\n", n_obs_per_imp))

# Pool across models
mate_results <- lapply(names(inla_fits), function(mod_name) {
  
  cat(sprintf("\nPooling MATE for %s...\n", mod_name))
  res       <- pool_mate(inla_fits[[mod_name]], 
                         coef_name = "urban_bin",
                         n_obs     = n_obs_per_imp)
  res$model <- mod_name
  res
  
}) %>% bind_rows() %>%
  select(model, MATE, SE, CI_low, CI_high, MATE_exp, lambda, df, per_imp_ests)

# Model fit comparison
fit_comparison <- lapply(names(inla_fits), function(mod_name) {
  
  dic_vals  <- sapply(1:5, function(m) inla_fits[[mod_name]][[m]]$dic$dic)
  waic_vals <- sapply(1:5, function(m) inla_fits[[mod_name]][[m]]$waic$waic)
  
  data.frame(
    model    = mod_name,
    DIC_mean = round(mean(dic_vals),  2),
    DIC_sd   = round(sd(dic_vals),    2),
    WAIC_mean = round(mean(waic_vals), 2),
    WAIC_sd   = round(sd(waic_vals),   2)
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

cat("MATE Comparison Across Models (Rubin's Rules)\n")
print(mate_results)

cat("Model Fit: Mean DIC and WAIC Across Imputations\n")
print(fit_comparison)

cat("\nInterpretation guide:\n")
for (i in 1:nrow(mate_results)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "  %s: Urban vs Rural MATE = %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f, %.4f) | exp = %.3f | lambda = %.4f\n",
    mate_results$model[i],
    mate_results$MATE[i],
    mate_results$CI_low[i],
    mate_results$CI_high[i],
    mate_results$MATE_exp[i],
    mate_results$lambda[i]
  ))
}

saveRDS(inla_fits,     "inla_fits_all.RDS")
saveRDS(mate_results,  "mate_results.RDS")
write.csv(mate_results,   "mate_results_all_models.csv",  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(fit_comparison, "inla_fit_comparison.csv",      row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n All INLA outputs saved.\n")
```

```{r visualization, cache=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

col_m1 <- "#3c0f5b"
col_m2 <- "#4a7a10"
col_m3 <- "#87067F"
cols   <- c(M1 = col_m1, M2 = col_m2, M3 = col_m3)

model_labels <- c(
  M1 = "M1: CEVAE-adjusted\n(Active dims 1, 7, 10)",
  M2 = "M2: CEVAE-adjusted\n(All 10 dims)",
  M3 = "M3: Naive baseline\n(No latent z)"
)

plot_df <- mate_results |>
  mutate(
    model       = factor(model, levels = c("M3", "M2", "M1")),
    model_label = factor(model_labels[as.character(model)],
                         levels = rev(model_labels))
  )

per_imp_df <- mate_results |>
  select(model, per_imp_ests) |>
  mutate(imp = strsplit(per_imp_ests, ", ")) |>
  unnest(imp) |>
  mutate(
    imp_num     = rep(1:5, times = nrow(mate_results)),
    imp_val     = as.numeric(imp),
    model       = factor(model, levels = c("M3", "M2", "M1")),
    model_label = factor(model_labels[as.character(model)],
                         levels = rev(model_labels))
  )

fit_df <- fit_comparison |>
  mutate(
    model       = factor(model, levels = c("M3", "M2", "M1")),
    model_label = factor(model_labels[as.character(model)],
                         levels = rev(model_labels)),
    label_dic   = sprintf("%.1f (\u00b1%.1f)", DIC_mean,  DIC_sd),
    label_waic  = sprintf("%.1f (\u00b1%.1f)", WAIC_mean, WAIC_sd)
  )

metrics_df <- fit_df |>
  left_join(plot_df |> select(model, lambda, df), by = "model")

p_forest <- ggplot(plot_df,
                   aes(x = MATE, y = model_label, color = model)) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +

  geom_point(data = per_imp_df,
             aes(x = imp_val, y = model_label, color = model),
             shape = 1, size = 3, alpha = 0.5,
             position = position_nudge(y = 0.18)) +

  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high),
                 linewidth = 1.4, alpha = 0.85) +

  geom_point(size = 5, shape = 18) +

  geom_text(aes(x = 0.36, 
                label = sprintf("%.4f (%.4f, %.4f)\nexp = %.3f",
                                MATE, CI_low, CI_high, MATE_exp)),
            hjust      = 0,
            size       = 4.5, 
            color      = "grey20",
            lineheight = 0.9) + 

  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(-0.02, 0.55), 
    breaks = seq(0, 0.5, 0.1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off", ylim = c(0.6, 3.8)) +
  labs(
    title    = "MATE of Urban vs Rural Residence on log(UIC)",
    subtitle = "Pooled across 5 imputations via Rubin's Rules | Open circles = per-imputation estimates",
    x        = "Marginal Average Treatment Effect (log scale)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 13, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 5, 30, 10) 
  )

col_positions <- c(0.25, 0.55, 0.78, 0.93)

p_metrics <- ggplot(metrics_df, aes(y = model_label)) +
  # Raise headers slightly higher
  annotate("text", x = col_positions, y = 3.8,
           label = c("DIC", "WAIC", "\u03bb", "df"),
           size = 5.5, fontface = "bold", color = "grey20", hjust = 0.5) +

  # Raise divider line to match
  annotate("segment", x = 0.10, xend = 1.10, y = Inf, yend = Inf,
           color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +

  # Values stay lower, so there’s space
  geom_text(aes(x = col_positions[1], label = label_dic, color = model),
            size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = col_positions[2], label = label_waic, color = model),
            size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = col_positions[3], color = model,
                label = sprintf("%.4f", lambda)),
            size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = col_positions[4], color = model,
                label = sprintf("%.0f", df)),
            size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +

  # Model labels on the left
  geom_text(aes(x = 0.03, label = as.character(model), color = model),
            size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5, fontface = "bold") +

  scale_color_manual(values = cols,
                     labels = model_labels,
                     name = "Model") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off", ylim = c(0.6, 3.55)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Model fit & imputation diagnostics",
    subtitle = "Mean (\u00b1SD) across 5 imputations | \u03bb = variance from missingness",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey40", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x     = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y     = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks      = element_blank(),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 5, 10, 5)
  )

ggsave("mate_forest.pdf", p_forest, width = 10, height = 5.5, device = cairo_pdf)

ggsave("mate_metrics.pdf", p_metrics, width = 7, height = 4, device = cairo_pdf)
```


### BYM

```{r bym-crosswalk, eval = RERUN_BYM, cache=FALSE}
library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(INLA)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

# ── 1. Load shapefile ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
shp <- st_read(
  "phl_adm_psa_namria_20231106_shp",
  layer = "phl_admbnda_adm2_psa_namria_20231106"
)

# ── 2. Corrected crosswalk from official ENNS codebook ─────────────────────────
crosswalk <- tribble(
  ~province_code, ~ADM2_PCODE,
  # Region I
  1,  "PH01028",   # Ilocos Norte
  2,  "PH01029",   # Ilocos Sur
  4,  "PH01055",   # Pangasinan
  # Region II
  5,  "PH02009",   # Batanes
  6,  "PH02015",   # Cagayan Province
  7,  "PH02031",   # Isabela Province
  8,  "PH02050",   # Nueva Vizcaya
  9,  "PH02057",   # Quirino
  # Region III (13=Angeles City, 17=Olongapo City are HUCs)
  10, "PH03008",   # Bataan       ← codebook: 10 = Bataan
  11, "PH03014",   # Bulacan      ← codebook: 11 = Bulacan
  12, "PH03049",   # Nueva Ecija  ← codebook: 12 = Nueva Ecija
  13, NA,          # Angeles City (HUC)
  14, "PH03054",   # Pampanga
  15, "PH03069",   # Tarlac
  16, "PH03071",   # Zambales
  17, NA,          # Olongapo City (HUC)
  18, "PH03077",   # Aurora
  # Region IV-A (codebook: 21=Laguna, 22=Quezon, 24=Rizal)
  21, "PH04034",   # Laguna
  22, "PH04056",   # Quezon Province
  24, "PH04058",   # Rizal
  # Region IV-B/MIMAROPA
  25, "PH17040",   # Marinduque
  27, "PH17052",   # Oriental Mindoro
  28, "PH17053",   # Palawan
  30, "PH17059",   # Romblon
  # Region V
  31, "PH05005",   # Albay
  32, "PH05016",   # Camarines Norte
  33, "PH05017",   # Camarines Sur
  34, "PH05020",   # Catanduanes
  35, "PH05041",   # Masbate
  36, "PH05062",   # Sorsogon
  # Region VI (40=Iloilo City HUC)
  37, "PH06004",   # Aklan
  38, "PH06006",   # Antique
  39, "PH06019",   # Capiz
  40, NA,          # Iloilo City (HUC)
  41, "PH06030",   # Iloilo Province
  44, "PH06079",   # Guimaras     ← codebook: 44 = Guimaras
  # Region VII (46=Mandaue, 47=Lapu-Lapu, 49=Cebu City are HUCs)
  45, "PH07012",   # Bohol
  46, NA,          # Mandaue City (HUC)
  47, NA,          # Lapu-Lapu City (HUC)
  48, "PH07022",   # Cebu Province ← codebook: 48 = Cebu
  49, NA,          # Cebu City (HUC)
  50, "PH07046",   # Negros Oriental
  51, "PH07061",   # Siquijor
  # Region VIII (53=Tacloban HUC)
  52, "PH08026",   # Eastern Samar
  53, NA,          # Tacloban City (HUC)
  54, "PH08037",   # Leyte
  55, "PH08048",   # Northern Samar
  56, "PH08060",   # Western Samar
  57, "PH08064",   # Southern Leyte
  58, "PH08078",   # Biliran
  # Region IX (60=Zamboanga City, 63=City of Isabela are HUCs)
  59, "PH09072",   # Zamboanga del Norte
  60, NA,          # Zamboanga City (HUC)
  61, "PH09073",   # Zamboanga del Sur
  62, "PH09083",   # Zamboanga Sibugay
  63, NA,          # City of Isabela (HUC)
  # Region X (65=Camiguin, 67=Iligan, 69=CDO are codebook codes)
  64, "PH10013",   # Bukidnon
  65, "PH10018",   # Camiguin     ← codebook: 65 = Camiguin
  66, "PH10035",   # Lanao del Norte
  67, NA,          # Iligan City (HUC)
  68, "PH10042",   # Misamis Occidental
  69, NA,          # Cagayan de Oro (HUC) ← codebook: 69 = CDO
  70, "PH10043",   # Misamis Oriental
  # Region XI (72=Davao City HUC)
  71, "PH11023",   # Davao del Norte
  72, NA,          # Davao City (HUC)
  74, "PH11025",   # Davao Oriental
  75, "PH11082",   # Davao de Oro (Compostela Valley)
  76, "PH11086",   # Davao Occidental
  # Region XII (79=GenSan, 82=Cotabato City are HUCs)
  77, "PH12047",   # North Cotabato
  78, "PH12063",   # South Cotabato
  79, NA,          # General Santos City (HUC)
  80, "PH12065",   # Sultan Kudarat
  81, "PH12080",   # Sarangani
  82, NA,          # Cotabato City (HUC)
  # NCR (codebook: 83=Manila, 84=Mandaluyong, ..., 99=Pasay)
  83, "PH13039",   # City of Manila
  84, "PH13074",   # Mandaluyong City
  85, "PH13074",   # San Juan City     → map to 2nd District
  86, "PH13074",   # Quezon City       → map to 2nd District
  87, "PH13074",   # City of Marikina  → map to 2nd District
  88, "PH13074",   # City of Pasig     → map to 2nd District
  89, "PH13075",   # Caloocan City     → map to 3rd District
  90, "PH13075",   # City of Malabon   → map to 3rd District
  91, "PH13075",   # City of Navotas   → map to 3rd District
  92, "PH13075",   # City of Valenzuela→ map to 3rd District
  93, "PH13076",   # Makati City       → map to 4th District
  94, "PH13076",   # Taguig City       → map to 4th District
  95, "PH13076",   # Las Piñas City    → map to 4th District
  96, "PH13076",   # City of Parañaque → map to 4th District
  97, "PH13076",   # Pateros           → map to 4th District
  98, "PH13076",   # City of Muntinlupa→ map to 4th District
  99, "PH13076",   # Pasay City        → map to 4th District
  # CAR (101=Baguio City HUC)
  100, "PH14001",  # Abra
  101, NA,         # Baguio City (HUC)
  102, "PH14011",  # Benguet
  104, "PH14032",  # Kalinga
  105, "PH14033",  # Mountain Province
  # BARMM
  107, "PH19007",  # Basilan
  108, "PH19036",  # Lanao del Sur
  109, "PH19044",  # Maguindanao  ← codebook: 109 = Maguindanao (not Sulu)
  # Caraga (111=Butuan City HUC)
  111, NA,         # Butuan City (HUC)
  112, "PH16002",  # Agusan del Norte
  113, "PH16003",  # Agusan del Sur
  114, "PH16067",  # Surigao del Norte
  115, "PH16068",  # Surigao del Sur
  116, "PH16085"   # Dinagat Islands
)
```


```{r bym-spatialre, eval = RERUN_BYM, cache=FALSE}
set.seed(0942)
# ── 3. Build spatial random effects from existing M1 fits ──────────────────────
inla_fits  <- readRDS("inla_fits_all.RDS")
inla_input <- readRDS("cevae_inla_input.RDS")

spatial_re <- lapply(1:5, function(m) {
  inla_fits[["M1"]][[m]]$summary.random$spatial_id %>%
    select(ID, mean) %>%
    rename(spatial_id = ID, re_mean = mean) %>%
    mutate(imputation = m)
}) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(spatial_id) %>%
  summarise(re_pooled = mean(re_mean), .groups = "drop")

# Province lookup: integer spatial_id → province_code
prov_lookup <- inla_input %>%
  filter(imputation_id == 1) %>%
  distinct(province_code) %>%
  arrange(province_code) %>%
  mutate(spatial_id = row_number())

# ── 4. Join RE to shapefile via crosswalk ──────────────────────────────────────
re_by_pcode <- prov_lookup %>%
  left_join(crosswalk, by = "province_code") %>%
  left_join(spatial_re, by = "spatial_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(ADM2_PCODE)) %>%
  group_by(ADM2_PCODE) %>%
  summarise(re_pooled = mean(re_pooled, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("Provinces matched:", nrow(re_by_pcode), "\n")
cat("No duplicates:", n_distinct(re_by_pcode$ADM2_PCODE) == nrow(re_by_pcode), "\n")

map_df_iid <- shp %>%
  left_join(re_by_pcode, by = "ADM2_PCODE")

# ── 5. Build adjacency graph for BYM2 ─────────────────────────────────────────
nb    <- poly2nb(map_df_iid, row.names = map_df_iid$ADM2_PCODE)
mat   <- nb2mat(nb, style = "B", zero.policy = TRUE)
graph <- inla.read.graph(mat)

# ── 6. Align spatial_id to graph node order ───────────────────────────────────

# Shapefile row order IS the graph node order
shp_order <- map_df_iid %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(ADM2_PCODE) %>%
  mutate(graph_node = row_number())

# Link graph nodes to province_code via crosswalk
node_lookup <- crosswalk %>%
  filter(!is.na(ADM2_PCODE)) %>%
  left_join(shp_order, by = "ADM2_PCODE") %>%
  filter(!is.na(graph_node)) %>%
  select(province_code, graph_node)

cat("Province codes matched to graph nodes:", nrow(node_lookup), "\n")

# Valid province codes — only those with a graph node
valid_province_codes <- node_lookup$province_code

# Report what gets dropped
dropped_codes <- inla_input %>%
  filter(imputation_id == 1) %>%
  distinct(province_code) %>%
  filter(!province_code %in% valid_province_codes)

cat("\nProvince codes dropped from BYM2 (HUCs or unmatched):\n")
print(dropped_codes)

# Observation-level drop summary
drop_summary <- inla_input %>%
  filter(imputation_id == 1) %>%
  summarise(
    total       = n(),
    dropped     = sum(!province_code %in% valid_province_codes),
    kept        = sum(province_code %in% valid_province_codes),
    pct_dropped = round(100 * mean(!province_code %in% valid_province_codes), 2)
  )
cat("\nObservation drop summary (imputation 1):\n")
print(drop_summary)

# ── 7. Data preparation functions ─────────────────────────────────────────────

# IID: keeps all observations, uses factor-based spatial_id
prepare_dat_iid <- function(dat_m) {
  dat_m %>%
    mutate(
      urban_bin     = as.numeric(urban == "Urban"),
      z_active_mean = rowMeans(across(c(z_mean_1, z_mean_7, z_mean_10))),
      z_std         = as.numeric(scale(z_active_mean)),
      age_std       = as.numeric(scale(age)),
      sex_bin       = as.numeric(sex == "Female"),
      educ_std      = as.numeric(scale(as.numeric(as.character(education)))),
      year_fact     = factor(survey_year),
      prov_id       = as.integer(factor(province_code)),
      region_id     = as.integer(factor(region)),
      spatial_id    = if (all(is.na(prov_id))) region_id else prov_id
    )
}

# BYM2: drops unmatched codes, uses graph-aligned spatial_id
prepare_dat_bym <- function(dat_m) {
  dat_m %>%
    filter(province_code %in% valid_province_codes) %>%
    mutate(
      urban_bin     = as.numeric(urban == "Urban"),
      z_active_mean = rowMeans(across(c(z_mean_1, z_mean_7, z_mean_10))),
      z_std         = as.numeric(scale(z_active_mean)),
      age_std       = as.numeric(scale(age)),
      sex_bin       = as.numeric(sex == "Female"),
      educ_std      = as.numeric(scale(as.numeric(as.character(education)))),
      year_fact     = factor(survey_year),
      spatial_id    = node_lookup$graph_node[
                        match(province_code, node_lookup$province_code)
                      ]
    )
}

# Final verification
test_bym <- inla_input %>% filter(imputation_id == 1) %>% prepare_dat_bym()
cat("\nBYM2 data verification:\n")
cat("  NAs in spatial_id :", sum(is.na(test_bym$spatial_id)), " (must be 0)\n")
cat("  spatial_id range  :", paste(range(test_bym$spatial_id), collapse = " – "), "\n")
cat("  graph$n           :", graph$n, "\n")
cat("  max <= graph$n    :", max(test_bym$spatial_id) <= graph$n, " (must be TRUE)\n")
cat("  Rows in BYM2 data :", nrow(test_bym), "\n")

# ── 8. Fit IID vs BYM2 across all 5 imputations ───────────────────────────────
M        <- 5
n_obs    <- 90515
df_m1    <- 29089

rubin_pool <- function(Q_vec, U_vec, df_adj) {
  Q_bar <- mean(Q_vec)
  U_bar <- mean(U_vec)
  B     <- var(Q_vec)
  T_var <- U_bar + (1 + 1/length(Q_vec)) * B
  SE_p  <- sqrt(T_var)
  t_c   <- qt(0.975, df = df_adj)
  data.frame(
    MATE     = round(Q_bar, 4),
    SE       = round(SE_p, 4),
    CI_low   = round(Q_bar - t_c * SE_p, 4),
    CI_high  = round(Q_bar + t_c * SE_p, 4),
    MATE_exp = round(exp(Q_bar), 4)
  )
}

pool_model <- function(fits, n_obs) {
  mate_tbl <- lapply(fits, function(f) {
    row <- f$summary.fixed["urban_bin", ]
    data.frame(Q = row$mean, U = row$sd^2)
  }) %>% bind_rows()

  M_f    <- nrow(mate_tbl)
  n_par  <- nrow(fits[[1]]$summary.fixed)
  B      <- var(mate_tbl$Q)
  U_bar  <- mean(mate_tbl$U)
  T_var  <- U_bar + (1 + 1/M_f) * B
  lambda <- (1 + 1/M_f) * B / T_var
  df_old <- (M_f - 1) / max(lambda, 1e-10)^2
  df_obs <- (n_obs - n_par) * (1 - lambda)
  df_adj <- (df_old * df_obs) / (df_old + df_obs)

  rubin_pool(mate_tbl$Q, mate_tbl$U, df_adj)
}

fits_iid            <- vector("list", M)   # full IID
fits_iid_restricted <- vector("list", M)   # IID on BYM2-eligible provinces only
fits_bym            <- vector("list", M)   # BYM2

dic_table <- data.frame(
  imputation    = 1:M,
  DIC_IID       = NA_real_,
  DIC_IID_restr = NA_real_,
  DIC_BYM       = NA_real_,
  WAIC_IID      = NA_real_,
  WAIC_IID_restr= NA_real_,
  WAIC_BYM      = NA_real_,
  n_iid         = NA_integer_,
  n_bym         = NA_integer_
)

for (m in 1:M) {
  cat(sprintf("\n── Imputation %d/%d ──\n", m, M))

  dat_iid <- inla_input %>% filter(imputation_id == m) %>% prepare_dat_iid()
  dat_bym <- inla_input %>% filter(imputation_id == m) %>% prepare_dat_bym()

  dic_table$n_iid[m] <- nrow(dat_iid)
  dic_table$n_bym[m] <- nrow(dat_bym)

  # ── IID full ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("  Fitting IID (full)...")
  fits_iid[[m]] <- inla(
    log_uic ~ urban_bin + z_std + age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
      f(spatial_id, model = "iid",
        hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1.0, 0.01)))),
    family            = "gaussian",
    data              = dat_iid,
    weights           = dat_iid$weight,
    control.compute   = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE),
    control.predictor = list(compute = FALSE)
  )
  cat(sprintf(" DIC = %.2f\n", fits_iid[[m]]$dic$dic))
  dic_table$DIC_IID[m]  <- fits_iid[[m]]$dic$dic
  dic_table$WAIC_IID[m] <- fits_iid[[m]]$waic$waic
  
# ── SPATIAL RANDOM EFFECTS ──────────────────────────────────
# Model: f(spatial_id, model = "iid", ...)
# 
# Interpretation:
#   NOT a general "unmeasured confounding" adjustment
#   Rather: Geographic variation in the urbanicity→iodine effect
#   Reasons for variation might include:
#     • Regional salt iodization programs (policy-level)
#     • Migration patterns (rural→urban diffusion)
#     • Local dietary practices (spatial clustering)
#     • Measured confounders vary geographically
#     • Unmeasured geographic confounders
# 
# Spatial REs estimate province-level deviations from average effect
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # ── IID restricted (same n as BYM2) ───────────────────────────────────────
  dat_iid_r <- dat_bym %>%
    mutate(spatial_id = as.integer(factor(province_code)))

  cat("  Fitting IID (restricted)...")
  fits_iid_restricted[[m]] <- inla(
    log_uic ~ urban_bin + z_std + age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
      f(spatial_id, model = "iid",
        hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1.0, 0.01)))),
    family            = "gaussian",
    data              = dat_iid_r,
    weights           = dat_iid_r$weight,
    control.compute   = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE),
    control.predictor = list(compute = FALSE)
  )
  cat(sprintf(" DIC = %.2f\n", fits_iid_restricted[[m]]$dic$dic))
  dic_table$DIC_IID_restr[m]  <- fits_iid_restricted[[m]]$dic$dic
  dic_table$WAIC_IID_restr[m] <- fits_iid_restricted[[m]]$waic$waic

  # ── BYM2 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("  Fitting BYM2...")
  fits_bym[[m]] <- inla(
    log_uic ~ urban_bin + z_std + age_std + sex_bin + educ_std + year_fact +
      f(spatial_id, model = "bym2", graph = graph,
        adjust.for.con.comp = TRUE,
        hyper = list(
          phi  = list(prior = "pc", param = c(0.5, 0.5)),
          prec = list(prior = "pc.prec", param = c(1.0, 0.01))
        )),
    family            = "gaussian",
    data              = dat_bym,
    weights           = dat_bym$weight,
    control.compute   = list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE),
    control.predictor = list(compute = FALSE)
  )
  cat(sprintf(" DIC = %.2f\n", fits_bym[[m]]$dic$dic))
  dic_table$DIC_BYM[m]  <- fits_bym[[m]]$dic$dic
  dic_table$WAIC_BYM[m] <- fits_bym[[m]]$waic$waic
}

# ── Pool MATE estimates ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
n_bym_obs <- round(mean(dic_table$n_bym))

mate_iid   <- pool_model(fits_iid,            n_obs)
mate_iid_r <- pool_model(fits_iid_restricted, n_bym_obs)
mate_bym   <- pool_model(fits_bym,            n_bym_obs)

cat("\n═══ MODEL COMPARISON SUMMARY ═══\n")

comparison_fair <- data.frame(
  Model     = c("IID (full n)", "IID (matched n)", "BYM2"),
  n         = c(n_obs, n_bym_obs, n_bym_obs),
  MATE      = c(mate_iid$MATE,     mate_iid_r$MATE,    mate_bym$MATE),
  CI_low    = c(mate_iid$CI_low,   mate_iid_r$CI_low,  mate_bym$CI_low),
  CI_high   = c(mate_iid$CI_high,  mate_iid_r$CI_high, mate_bym$CI_high),
  MATE_exp  = c(mate_iid$MATE_exp, mate_iid_r$MATE_exp,mate_bym$MATE_exp),
  DIC_mean  = c(mean(dic_table$DIC_IID),
                mean(dic_table$DIC_IID_restr),
                mean(dic_table$DIC_BYM)),
  delta_DIC = c(NA, NA,
                mean(dic_table$DIC_BYM) - mean(dic_table$DIC_IID_restr))
)

print(comparison_fair)
cat(sprintf("\nFair ΔDIC (BYM2 − IID matched): %.2f\n",
            mean(dic_table$DIC_BYM) - mean(dic_table$DIC_IID_restr)))
cat("Negative = BYM2 fits better on same data\n")
print(dic_table)

write.csv(comparison_fair, "iid_vs_bym2_fair_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dic_table,       "iid_vs_bym2_dic_by_imputation.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nAll outputs saved.\n")
```



```{r bym-poolre, cache=FALSE}
# ── 10. Extract and pool BYM2 spatial random effects ──────────────────────────
set.seed(0942)
n_nodes <- graph$n

spatial_re_bym <- lapply(1:M, function(m) {
  fits_bym[[m]]$summary.random$spatial_id %>%
    slice(1:n_nodes) %>%
    filter(ID %in% node_lookup$graph_node) %>%
    select(ID, mean) %>%
    rename(graph_node = ID, re_mean = mean) %>%
    mutate(imputation = m)
}) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(graph_node) %>%
  summarise(re_bym_pooled = mean(re_mean), .groups = "drop")

cat("BYM2 spatial RE extracted:", nrow(spatial_re_bym), "nodes\n")

# ── 11. Map BYM2 RE back to ADM2_PCODE via node_lookup ───────────────────────

stopifnot(
  "ADM2_PCODE" %in% names(shp_order),
  "graph_node" %in% names(shp_order)
)

node_to_pcode <- shp_order %>%
  select(ADM2_PCODE, graph_node)

re_bym_by_pcode <- spatial_re_bym %>%
  left_join(node_to_pcode, by = "graph_node") %>%
  filter(!is.na(ADM2_PCODE)) %>%
  group_by(ADM2_PCODE) %>%
  summarise(re_bym_pooled = mean(re_bym_pooled, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("BYM2 RE matched to PCODEs:", nrow(re_bym_by_pcode), "\n")

# ── 12. Merge IID and BYM2 RE for comparison map ──────────────────────────────

re_compare <- re_by_pcode %>%
  rename(re_iid_pooled = re_pooled) %>%
  left_join(re_bym_by_pcode, by = "ADM2_PCODE")

cat("Provinces with IID RE :", sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_iid_pooled)), "\n")
cat("Provinces with BYM2 RE:", sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_bym_pooled)), "\n")

map_compare <- shp %>%
  left_join(re_compare, by = "ADM2_PCODE")

# ── 13. Shared fill scale ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

re_range <- range(
  c(re_compare$re_iid_pooled, re_compare$re_bym_pooled),
  na.rm = TRUE
)
re_limit <- max(abs(re_range))

fill_scale <- function() {
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#4a7a10",
    mid      = "#fdfbc8",
    high     = "#3c0f5b",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-re_limit, re_limit),
    na.value = "grey85",
    name     = "Spatial RE\n(log UIC)"
  )
}

# ── 14. Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

p_iid <- ggplot(map_compare) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = re_iid_pooled), color = "#fdfbc8", linewidth = 0.1) +
  fill_scale() +
  labs(
    title    = "IID Model",
    subtitle = "Independent spatial random effects"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_bym <- ggplot(map_compare) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = re_bym_pooled), color = "#fdfbc8", linewidth = 0.1) +
  fill_scale() +
  labs(
    title    = "BYM2 Model",
    subtitle = "Spatially structured + unstructured RE"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

combined_map <- (p_iid | p_bym) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Spatial Random Effects — IID vs BYM2",
    subtitle = "Posterior means pooled across M = 5 imputations | ENNS 2018–2021",
    caption  = sprintf(
      paste0(
        "Grey = missing data. IID: %d provinces. BYM2: %d provinces ",
        "(20 province codes dropped — HUCs or graph-unmatched).\n",
        "Boundary: NAMRIA/PSA via HDX"
      ),
      sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_iid_pooled)),
      sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_bym_pooled))
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey50")
    )
  )

ggsave("iid_vs_bym2_spatial_map.pdf", combined_map,
       width = 16, height = 10, device = "pdf")
cat("iid_vs_bym2_spatial_map.pdf saved!\n")
```

```{r diff-map, cache=FALSE}
set.seed(0942)
# ── 15. Difference map (BYM2 − IID) ──────────────────────────────────────────
re_compare <- re_compare %>%
  mutate(re_diff = re_bym_pooled - re_iid_pooled)

map_compare <- shp %>%
  left_join(re_compare, by = "ADM2_PCODE")

diff_limit <- max(abs(re_compare$re_diff), na.rm = TRUE)

p_diff <- ggplot(map_compare) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = re_diff, coord_sf(expand = FALSE)), 
          color = "#fdfbc8", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#4a7a10",   # green = BYM2 pulled negative
    mid      = "#fdfbc8",
    high     = "#3c0f5b",   # purple = BYM2 pulled positive
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-diff_limit, diff_limit),
    na.value = "grey85",
    name     = "Δ RE\n(BYM2 − IID)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Difference (BYM2 − IID)",
    subtitle = "Positive = BYM2 higher; Negative = BYM2 lower"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# ── 16. Scatter plot: IID vs BYM2 RE with province labels ────────────────────

library(ggrepel)

# Label only provinces where the difference is large
label_threshold <- quantile(abs(re_compare$re_diff), 0.85, na.rm = TRUE)

scatter_data <- re_compare %>%
  filter(!is.na(re_iid_pooled), !is.na(re_bym_pooled)) %>%
  left_join(
    shp %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% select(ADM2_PCODE, ADM2_EN),
    by = "ADM2_PCODE"
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(abs(re_diff) >= label_threshold, ADM2_EN, NA))

p_scatter <- ggplot(scatter_data, aes(x = re_iid_pooled, y = re_bym_pooled)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +   # y = x reference line
  geom_point(aes(color = re_diff), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = label),
    size = 2.8, max.overlaps = 20,
    segment.color = "grey60", segment.size = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#4a7a10", mid = "#fdfbc8", high =  "#3c0f5b",
    midpoint = 0, name = "Δ RE\n(BYM2 − IID)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "IID vs BYM2 Spatial Random Effects",
    subtitle = "Points on the dashed line = no change; deviation = spatial smoothing effect",
    x        = "IID RE (log UIC)",
    y        = "BYM2 RE (log UIC)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# ── 17. Save both ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

combined_map3 <- (p_diff) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Spatial Random Effects — IID vs BYM2",
    subtitle = "Posterior means pooled across M = 5 imputations | ENNS 2018–2021",
    caption  = sprintf(
      paste0(
        "Grey = missing data. IID: %d provinces. BYM2: %d provinces ",
        "(20 province codes dropped — HUCs or graph-unmatched).\n",
        "Boundary: NAMRIA/PSA via HDX"
      ),
      sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_iid_pooled)),
      sum(!is.na(re_compare$re_bym_pooled))
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey50"),
      plot.margin = margin(t=-10)
    )
  )

ggsave("iid_vs_bym2_spatial_map3.pdf", combined_map3,
       width = 15, height = 10, device = "pdf")

ggsave("iid_vs_bym2_scatter.pdf", p_scatter,
       width = 8, height = 7, device = "pdf")

cat("iid_vs_bym2_spatial_map3.pdf saved!\n")
cat("iid_vs_bym2_scatter.pdf saved!\n")
```

```{r save-inla-workspace, include=FALSE}
workspace_path <- "~/thesis_char/Thesis/inla_workspace.RData"

objects_to_save <- c(
  # INLA M1, M2, M3 fits & results
  "inla_fits",           # Main 3-model comparison fits
  "mate_results",        # Pooled MATE estimates
  "fit_comparison",      # DIC/WAIC comparison
  
  # BYM spatial components (CRITICAL for mapping)
  "fits_iid",            # IID full fits
  "fits_iid_restricted", # IID restricted fits
  "fits_bym",            # BYM2 fits
  
  # BYM results & comparison
  "mate_iid",            # Pooled MATE (IID full)
  "mate_iid_r",          # Pooled MATE (IID restricted)
  "mate_bym",            # Pooled MATE (BYM2)
  "comparison_fair",     # Fair comparison table
  "dic_table",           # DIC/WAIC by imputation
  
  # Spatial graph metadata (CRITICAL - needed if sensitivity reuses these)
  "graph",               # Adjacency graph for BYM2
  "node_lookup",         # Graph node → province_code mapping
  "shp_order",           # Shapefile row order
  "valid_province_codes",# Provinces matched to graph
  "nb",                  # Neighbor list
  "crosswalk"            # Province code crosswalk
)

# Only save what exists
objects_to_save <- objects_to_save[objects_to_save %in% ls()]

save(list = objects_to_save, file = workspace_path)

cat("✓ INLA workspace saved:", round(file.info(workspace_path)$size / 1e6, 1), "MB\n")
cat("Objects saved:", length(objects_to_save), "\n")
```
